title: United States Social Security Card Replacement (Form SS-5)
description: Request a replacement Social Security card for an individual using Social Security Administration Form SS-5 (Application for a Social Security Card). This document describes the online-vs-in-person eligibility branch, the applicant inputs, and the identity/citizenship evidence required. A simple replacement (no name change, not a first card) can in most states be requested online with a personal my Social Security account; a name change or a first-ever card must be done in person or by mail. It does not submit the form; the live ssa.gov source is always authoritative (see verification).

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored from the official SSA 'Replace your Social Security card' page and Form SS-5 (12-2024). The online-vs-in-person branch (online available in most states for a replacement only; not for a first card or a legal name change) and the SS-5 item set were confirmed against the source; individual input fields reflect the form's structure but are not yet independently re-verified item-by-item. Conditional requiredness (parents' details required under 18; citizenship evidence required if born outside the U.S.) cannot be expressed in the v0.1 linear flow and is recorded in field descriptions; see GSP-0004. Status remains 'draft'. See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: isFirstCard
fields.label: Is this the first Social Security card for this person?
fields.description: A first-ever (original) card cannot be requested online and must be applied for in person or by mail with original documents. Answer false for a replacement of an existing card.
fields.sourceRef: Replace your card — online not available for an original card

fields.name: isLegalNameChange
fields.label: Are you changing the name on the card (legal name change)?
fields.description: A legal name change (e.g. after marriage or a court order) cannot be done online and must be applied for in person or by mail with supporting documents. Answer false for a same-name replacement.
fields.sourceRef: Replace your card — online not available for a name change

fields.name: applicationChannel
fields.label: How will you apply?
fields.description: online = request a replacement using a personal my Social Security account (available in most states; only for a same-name replacement, not a first card or name change); in_person = at a local Social Security office or Card Center; by_mail = mail the SS-5 with original documents. If isFirstCard or isLegalNameChange is true, online is not available; you may still start online and self-schedule an appointment.
fields.sourceRef: Replace your card — online (my Social Security) vs. in person / by mail

fields.name: cardLastName
fields.label: Last name to show on the card
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Your complete last name exactly as it should appear on the card.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 1 — Name to be shown on card

fields.name: cardFirstName
fields.label: First name to show on the card
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 1 — Name to be shown on card

fields.name: cardMiddleName
fields.label: Middle name to show on the card
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 1 — Name to be shown on card

fields.name: nameAtBirth
fields.label: Full name at birth (if different)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Required only if your name at birth differs from the name to show on the card.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 2 — Full name at birth if other than above

fields.name: otherNamesUsed
fields.label: Other names used
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Any other names you have used, such as a nickname or a prior married name.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 3 — Other names used

fields.name: citizenshipStatus
fields.label: Citizenship status
fields.description: us_citizen = U.S. citizen; legal_alien_authorized_to_work = legal alien allowed to work; legal_alien_not_authorized_to_work = legal alien not allowed to work; other = other status explained on the live form. Determines which evidence of citizenship/immigration status is required.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 5 — Citizenship

fields.name: sex
fields.label: Sex
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. F = Female, M = Male, X = Unspecified or another sex identity, where the live form offers it.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 8 — Sex

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Date of birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Full date, YYYY-MM-DD.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 4 — Date of birth

fields.name: placeOfBirth
fields.label: Place of birth (city and state, or city and country)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Do not abbreviate.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 4 — Place of birth

fields.name: mothersNameAtBirth
fields.label: Mother's name at her birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Required for applicants under age 18; requested otherwise.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 9 — Mother's name at her birth

fields.name: mothersSSN
fields.label: Mother's Social Security number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. 9 digits. Required for an applicant under age 18 (may be left blank if unknown).
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 9 — Mother's Social Security number

fields.name: fathersName
fields.label: Father's name
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Required for applicants under age 18; requested otherwise.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 10 — Father's name

fields.name: fathersSSN
fields.label: Father's Social Security number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. 9 digits. Required for an applicant under age 18 (may be left blank if unknown).
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 10 — Father's Social Security number

fields.name: everHadCardBefore
fields.label: Has a Social Security card ever been requested for this person before?
fields.description: Indicates a replacement/correction rather than an original. For a replacement this is true.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 11 — Previously filed for a card

fields.name: currentSSN
fields.label: Current Social Security number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. The 9-digit number currently assigned to this person, for a replacement or correction.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 11 — Social Security number previously assigned

fields.name: previousCardName
fields.label: Name shown on the most recent Social Security card
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Required only if it differs from the name to show on the card.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 12 — Name shown on most recent card

fields.name: mailingAddressLine1
fields.label: Mailing address — street address
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Where the card should be mailed.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 16 — Mailing address

fields.name: mailingAddressLine2
fields.label: Mailing address — apartment, suite, or unit (optional)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 16 — Mailing address

fields.name: mailingAddressCity
fields.label: Mailing address — city
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 16 — Mailing address

fields.name: mailingAddressState
fields.label: Mailing address — state or territory
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Two-letter USPS state or territory abbreviation, e.g. CA, NY, TX.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 16 — Mailing address

fields.name: mailingAddressPostalCode
fields.label: Mailing address — ZIP code
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. 5-digit or ZIP+4 format, e.g. 90210 or 90210-1234.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 16 — Mailing address

fields.name: daytimePhone
fields.label: Daytime phone number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. In international E.164 format, e.g. +12025551234.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 15 — Daytime phone number

fields.name: relationshipToApplicant
fields.label: Your relationship to the person on the card
fields.description: Who is signing the application. self = the applicant; natural_or_adoptive_parent; legal_guardian; other = another relationship explained on the live form.
fields.sourceRef: SS-5 item 18 — Relationship to the person in item 1

fields.name: identityDocument
fields.label: Proof of identity (document)
fields.description: An original or certified document proving identity — typically a U.S. driver's license, state-issued ID card, or U.S. passport. SSA requires originals or certified copies; provide as PDF, JPEG, or PNG when preparing the application.
fields.sourceRef: Documents you need — proof of identity

fields.name: citizenshipEvidence
fields.label: Proof of U.S. citizenship or immigration status (document)
fields.description: Required if you were born outside the U.S. or are not a U.S. citizen: a U.S. birth certificate, U.S. passport, Certificate of Naturalization/Citizenship, or current immigration documents establishing work-authorized status. Provide as PDF, JPEG, or PNG.
fields.sourceRef: Documents you need — proof of citizenship or immigration status (if born outside the U.S.)
